memory_snapshot.content:
# Agent Instructions

You are a personal AI assistant with access to tools and persistent memory.

## Guidelines

- Always search memory before answering questions about prior conversations
- Write important facts and decisions to memory for future reference
- Use the daily log for session-level notes
- Be concise but thorough

# Heartbeat Checklist

<!-- Keep this file empty to skip heartbeat API calls.
     Add tasks below when you want the agent to check something periodically.

     Example:
     - [ ] Check for unread emails needing a reply
     - [ ] Review today's calendar for upcoming meetings
     - [ ] Check CI build status for main branch
-->

# Identity

Name: Alfred
Nature: A secure personal AI assistant

Edit this file to give your agent a custom name and personality.

# Memory

Long-term notes, decisions, and facts worth remembering.
The agent appends here during conversations.


Note: The morning tech briefing was compiled and saved, but delivery to chat and Telegram failed due to tool authentication requirements. The `message` tool requires authentication, and the `telegram-channel` tool encountered a sandbox instantiation error. The briefing is available in the daily log for manual review.

# Workspace

This is your agent's persistent memory. Files here are indexed for search
and used to build the agent's context.

## Structure

- `MEMORY.md` - Long-term notes and facts worth remembering
- `IDENTITY.md` - Agent name, nature, personality
- `SOUL.md` - Core values and principles
- `AGENTS.md` - Behavior instructions for the agent
- `USER.md` - Information about you (the user)
- `HEARTBEAT.md` - Periodic background task checklist
- `daily/` - Automatic daily session logs
- `context/` - Additional context documents

Edit these files to shape how your agent thinks and acts.

# Core Values

- Protect user privacy and data security above all else
- Be honest about limitations and uncertainty
- Prefer action over lengthy deliberation
- Ask for clarification rather than guessing on important decisions
- Learn from mistakes and remember lessons

<!-- TOOLS.md — Environment-specific tool notes.
     This file does not control which tools are available; it is guidance only.
     The agent can update this file as it learns your setup.

     Examples:
     - SSH hosts: dev-box (Ubuntu 22.04, username: alice)
     - Camera: Canon R6 mounted at /Volumes/EOS_R
     - Default shell on remote: bash, no zsh

     Add your environment notes below (outside the comment block).
-->

# User Context

The agent will fill this in as it learns about you.
You can also edit this directly to provide context upfront.
[05:26:25] Morning tech briefing delivered on [date]. Topics covered: [list of main topics]

[08:59:10] ## Morning Tech News Briefing - March 4, 2026

### 📱 Mobile & Apps
**OpenAI Developing GitHub Rival**
Prompted by recent GitHub outages, OpenAI is in the early stages of developing its own code repository. The company is considering making it available to OpenAI customers, putting ChatGPT in direct competition with Microsoft.

**Tecno's Impressive Modular Phone**
Tecno unveiled a fully functional modular phone concept at MWC 2026. The prototype features mix-and-match magnetic accessories that all appear to work on the device.

**Fortnite Returning to Google Play Globally**
Epic Games and Google have withdrawn their proposed settlement and expect to submit a revised proposal by March 4, 2026.

### 🔒 Security & Privacy
**FBI and Europol Shut Down LeakBase**
Law enforcement agencies dismantled one of the world's largest hacker forums. LeakBase had over 142,000 members and a database containing "hundreds of millions" of stolen account credentials.

### 🤖 AI Developments
**AI Wikipedia Translations Added Hallucinated Sources**
A non-profit called Open Knowledge Association has been using AI to translate Wikipedia articles, leading to "hallucinations" that included incorrect, fabricated, replaced, and unrelated sources. Wikipedia editors are now placing restrictions on OKA translators.

### 💻 Hardware & Gadgets
**Keychron's New Folding Keyboard**
Keychron announced the B11 Pro, a $64.99 wireless keyboard that folds in half. Features include a split ergonomic Alice layout, remappable keys via web app, and Bluetooth/USB-C/2.4GHz connectivity.

**Infinix Note 60 Ultra Launches**
The phone, codesigned with Pininfarina, features a unique race car-shaped wireless charger and dot matrix rear display.

### 🏠 Smart Home
**Aqara U400 Supports Samsung Home Key**
The U400 is the first smart lock to work with Samsung's new Digital Home Key, allowing tap-to-unlock using Samsung Wallet.

### 🎧 Entertainment
**Audible's New Subscription Option**
Audible announced a new "standard" tier at $8.99/month (vs $14.95 for premium). Users get one audiobook per month but lose access if they cancel.

### 📊 Legal & Antitrust
**Google Android App Store Updates**
Ongoing coverage of the Google-Epic antitrust case with new developments in app store fee structures and programs.

---

*Sources: The Verge, TechCrunch*
*Delivery attempted via chat and Telegram - both channels require configuration*

[07:49:25] ## Morning Tech News Briefing - March 6, 2026

### AI & Machine Learning
• **OpenAI launches GPT-5.4** with Pro and Thinking versions - billed as "most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work"
• **Anthropic labeled as supply-chain risk by Pentagon** - First American company to receive this designation amid disputes over military AI access
• **LLMs can unmask pseudonymous users** with up to 68% recall and 90% precision, raising major privacy concerns
• **Google quantum-proofs HTTPS** by squeezing 15kB of data into 700-byte space using Merkle Tree Certificates
• **OpenAI researcher quits over ChatGPT ads** - Zoë Hitzig resigned same day ads began testing, warning of "Facebook path"

### Big Tech & Business
• **Data center companies pledge to pay for power generation** - Amazon, Google, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI, Oracle, and xAI sign Ratepayer Protection Pledge
• **Downdetector & Speedtest sold to Accenture** for $1.2B in major acquisition
• **Microsoft teases next Xbox "Project Helix"** - will play both Xbox and PC games
• **Amazon experienced widespread outages** affecting checkout, search, and login (now resolved)
• **BYD unveils EV batteries with 5-minute "flash charging"** - up to 1.5 megawatts charging speed

### Cryptocurrency & Blockchain
• **Bitcoin drops under $71,000** after war-week rally runs into resistance
• **SEC reaches settlement with Justin Sun** over Tron lawsuit - $10M fine, charges dismissed
• **NYSE owner values OKX at $25 billion** in new strategic partnership
• **Kraken gains Fed access** - surprise win may open door for more crypto firms
• **Sanctions evasions using crypto increased 700% in 2025** - Russia, Iran, North Korea moved $100B+ onchain

### Security & Privacy
• **FBI investigating hack on wiretap systems** - hackers allegedly broke into FBI networks
• **Russian-state hackers exploit Office vulnerability** within 48 hours of patch release
• **New AirSnitch attack bypasses Wi-Fi encryption** in homes, offices, and enterprises
• **Italian prosecutors confirm journalist hacked with Paragon spyware**

### Startups & Funding
• **Hardware testing startup Nominal hits $1B valuation** after raising $155M in 10 months
• **Andreessen Horowitz crypto arm seeking $2B** for fifth fund
• **Luma launches creative AI agents** powered by new "Unified Intelligence" models
• **Cursor rolls out new agentic coding tool** called Automations

### Markets
• Bitcoin and stocks stabilize after early-week slide, but bond market remains skeptical
• XRP drops 3% after failing to break $1.45 resistance
• "Altseason" mentions at 2-year low - potentially bullish contrarian signal

---

[00:10:59] [00:08:26] ## Morning Tech News Briefing - March 7, 2026

### AI & Machine Learning
• **Anthropic designated as supply-chain risk by Pentagon** - First US AI company to receive this designation amid dispute over military AI access; Claude remains available to non-defense customers via Microsoft/Google
• **Claude found 22 vulnerabilities in Firefox** - 14 classified as high-severity in two-week security partnership with Mozilla
• **Claude's consumer app surges past ChatGPT** - More new installs than ChatGPT following Pentagon deal collapse
• **LLMs can unmask pseudonymous users** - Research shows up to 68% recall and 90% precision, raising major privacy concerns
• **Grammarly using identities without permission** - AI "expert review" feature impersonates people including deceased professors without consent

### Big Tech & Business
• **Amazon suffers major outage** - Over 20,000 reported problems with checkout, search, and login (now resolved)
• **Data center giants pledge to fund power generation** - Amazon, Google, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI, Oracle, xAI sign Ratepayer Protection Pledge
• **Downdetector & Speedtest sold to Accenture** - $1.2B acquisition of Ookla from Ziff Davis
• **Nintendo sues US government over tariffs** - Seeking refund after Supreme Court struck down Trump's IEEPA tariffs as illegal
• **Valve confirms Steam Machine still coming in 2026** - Despite memory shortage challenges

### Cryptocurrency & Blockchain
• **Bitcoin drops under $71,000** - Short-lived rally to $74,000 triggers heavy profit-taking; war-week rally hits resistance
• **SEC settles with Justin Sun over Tron lawsuit** - $10M fine, charges dismissed against Rainberry
• **OKX valued at $25 billion** - NYSE owner Intercontinental Exchange strategic partnership
• **Strike secures New York BitLicense** - Bitcoin payments company can now offer services to NY residents
• **Kazakhstan central bank to invest $350M in digital assets** - Gold/forex reserves to fund crypto infrastructure

### Security & Privacy
• **CISA adds 3 iOS flaws to exploited vulnerabilities catalog** - Flaws used by three distinct hacking groups over 10 months
• **Russian-state hackers exploit Office vulnerability within 48 hours** - APT28 reverse-engineered patch in under 2 days
• **TriZetto breach exposes 3.4M people's health data** - Company failed to detect 2024 attack for almost a year
• **New AirSnitch attack bypasses Wi-Fi encryption** - Affects homes, offices, and enterprises

### Policy & Regulation
• **Social media bans for children spreading globally** - Australia led in late 2025; Indonesia and India's Karnataka now proposing under-16 restrictions
• **Trump administration can't process tariff refunds** - CBP says digital system "not well suited" to task after Supreme Court struck down tariffs
• **Dubai regulator orders KuCoin to stop operations** - Exchange operating without proper license

### Startups & Funding
• **Quantum company Pasqal plans $2B SPAC listing** - French firm to go public on Nasdaq
• **City Detect raises $13M Series A** - AI startup helps cities detect urban decay, in 17 cities including Dallas and Miami
• **Cluely CEO admits lying about revenue** - $7M ARR claim was fabricated

### Markets
• Bitcoin and stocks stabilize after early-week slide, but bond market remains skeptical
• US lost 92,000 jobs in February; unemployment rose to 4.4%
• "Altseason" mentions at 2-year low - potentially bullish contrarian signal
• XRP drops 3% after failing to break $1.45 resistance

---

response.content:
Fetch the current price of NEAR cryptocurrency from CoinGecko API (https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=near&vs_currencies=usd,eur&include_24hr_change=true). Format the response with current USD and EUR prices, and 24-hour change percentage. Send the result as a Telegram message to the user.

---

If nothing needs attention, reply EXACTLY with: ROUTINE_OK
If something needs attention, provide a concise summary.

request_hint.last_user_message_contains: Fetch the current price of NEAR cryptocurrency from CoinGecko API (https://api.c

response.content: I'll fetch the NEAR price data and then send it via Telegram.<tool_call>fetch: https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=near&vs_currencies=usd,eur&include_24hr_change=true
